title: Vaccinium auriculatum
taxonomy: Vaccinium auriculatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a member of the Vaccinium genus, which includes various types of blueberries and cranberries. The plant is native to certain parts of China. Vaccinium auriculatum is closely related to other Vaccinium species, such as Vaccinium corymbosum and Vaccinium angustifolium. The exact classification of Vaccinium auriculatum is further detailed in botanical literature.

intro: Vaccinium auriculatum, commonly known as the golden-leaf blueberry, is a species of shrub native to the eastern Himalayas and southern China. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, dysentery, and digestive issues, due to its antidiarrheal and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium auriculatum include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vaccinium auriculatum is often prepared as a decoction or made into a tea, and it is also used to produce jams, preserves, and other herbal supplements.
